title: Sallaum Lines West Africa Service — March/April 2026 Schedule Update
summary: Sallaum Lines confirms the WA-1 service rotation for March–April 2026. GREAT ABIDJAN departs Baltimore 18 March, ETA Lagos 2 April. GRANDE ANGOLA next sailing departs Baltimore 4 April.
body: Sallaum Lines West Africa Service — Schedule Bulletin March 2026. The WA-1 service continues with two-vessel rotation. GREAT ABIDJAN (IMO 9935040) departed Baltimore (USBAL) on 18 March 2026 with 42 ro-ro units. Current declared ETA at Lagos (Tin Can Island Terminal) remains 2 April 2026. Customers are reminded that Lagos discharge operations typically experience 2–5 day port waiting times before berthing. The following sailing, GRANDE ANGOLA, is scheduled to depart Baltimore on 4 April 2026 with ETA Lagos 20 April. Note: Sallaum advises all customers to ensure Form M and PAAR documentation is lodged at least 7 days before vessel ETA. Contact your freight forwarder for Nigerian customs pre-clearance. Service reliability on this lane Q1 2026: 78% on-time within 48 hours of declared ETA.

title: GREAT ABIDJAN — Voyage Notice & Intermediate Port Call Update
summary: GREAT ABIDJAN confirms intermediate call at Cotonou (Benin) before Lagos. Estimated Cotonou arrival 30 March. Lagos arrival revised to 3 April due to Cotonou discharge volume.
body: Voyage Notice — GREAT ABIDJAN — March 2026 Voyage. Vessel GREAT ABIDJAN (IMO: 9935040, MMSI: 357123000) is currently en route from Baltimore to West Africa. The vessel will make an intermediate call at Cotonou, Benin (BJCOO) before proceeding to Lagos, Nigeria (NGLOS — Tin Can Island Terminal). Updated port call schedule: Cotonou ETA 30 March 2026, ETD 31 March 2026. Lagos ETA revised to 3 April 2026 (previously 2 April). The revision is due to additional discharge volume at Cotonou. Customers with cargo on this sailing should note: Tin Can Island berth allocation is pending confirmation with the Nigerian Ports Authority. Anchorage waiting time at Lagos currently estimated at 2–4 days. Realistic vehicle delivery date from terminal: 7–10 April 2026, subject to customs clearance.

title: Sallaum Lines — Q2 2026 West Africa Service Notice
summary: Sallaum Lines announces increase to bi-weekly frequency on US–West Africa lane starting June 2026. New vessel GRANDE SENEGAL to join rotation.
body: Service Enhancement Notice — Q2 2026. Sallaum Lines is pleased to announce an upgrade to the West Africa service beginning June 2026. The WA-1 service will transition from a monthly to bi-weekly sailing frequency. A third vessel, GRANDE SENEGAL (IMO 9941122), will join the Baltimore–Cotonou–Lagos rotation. This enhancement reflects growing demand for ro-ro capacity on the US–West Africa corridor. Customers can expect more flexible booking windows and reduced transit times due to increased port frequency. Pre-booking for Q2 2026 is now open. Customers with regular volumes are advised to contact their Sallaum account manager for capacity allocation. Note: The schedule enhancement is subject to final Nigerian Ports Authority berthing slot confirmation.

title: Sallaum — Cargo Receipt Deadline Notice (April 2026 Sailing)
summary: Cargo receipt deadline for April 4 sailing from Baltimore is 1 April 2026 at 17:00 EST. Late arrivals will be rolled to next available sailing.
body: Cargo Receipt Deadline — April 2026 Baltimore Sailing. Sallaum Lines advises all customers that cargo receipt for the GRANDE ANGOLA voyage departing Baltimore on 4 April 2026 closes on 1 April 2026 at 17:00 Eastern Time. Vehicles must be delivered to the Sallaum Baltimore terminal (South Locust Point Marine Terminal) no later than this time. Vehicles not received by the deadline will be automatically rebooked on the next available sailing (estimated departure 2 May 2026). Documentation requirements: Bill of Lading instructions must be submitted by 2 April 2026 at 12:00. Export customs clearance (AES filing) must be completed 24 hours before vessel departure. Contact ops@sallaum.com for cargo status queries.

title: Sallaum Lines — Lagos Port Advisory (Ongoing Congestion)
summary: Sallaum advises customers that Lagos Tin Can Island Terminal is experiencing elevated congestion as of late March 2026. Average anchorage wait time is 4–6 days before berthing.
body: Port Advisory — Lagos Tin Can Island Terminal — March 2026. Sallaum Lines is aware of elevated congestion at Tin Can Island Terminal, Lagos. As of 28 March 2026, vessels are experiencing an average anchorage wait of 4–6 days before receiving a berth. Contributing factors include: a backlog of containers displacing ro-ro berth priority, NPA (Nigerian Ports Authority) labour action (resolved 25 March but backlog persists), and elevated import volumes ahead of the Easter public holiday window. Impact on GREAT ABIDJAN: the vessel's revised ETA of 3 April may be further delayed. Sallaum estimates realistic berth date of 6–8 April. Vehicle release from terminal: subject to customs clearance which typically takes 3–7 additional working days. Customers are advised to ensure all pre-arrival documentation is complete to avoid further delay. Monitor sallaum-lines.vercel.app for daily updates.
